reviews.review_description: before playing this game, I was unemployed and depressed. When I played this game, suddenly I got a job, I got taller by 2ft, and all the ladies started crowding me. 10/10 life changing inspirational work of art

I found this game at what I can only describe as the absolute lowest point of existence. I was homeless, unemployed, and spiritually folded like a lawn chair in a hurricane. The world had basically stopped loading for me. No updates. No patches. Just pain and lag.

Then I discovered this game.

From the moment I launched it, everything changed. The sky outside didn’t just look different—it felt different. Birds started flying in formation like they were rendering in higher FPS just for me. I swear even gravity felt more generous.

Within minutes, I was no longer a lost soul wandering the earth. I was a chosen player. The tutorial alone healed generational trauma I didn’t even know I had. The soundtrack didn’t just play—it repaired emotional damage in real time. I could feel my unemployment statistics actively decreasing in the background like a passive buff.

I started grinding the game with a level of dedication that shocked medical professionals who were no longer involved in my life because I had transcended the need for healthcare. Every quest I completed felt like I was simultaneously filing taxes, fixing the economy, and emotionally supporting my past self through a breakup I never had.

Before I knew it, I had a job offer appear in real life like an in-game loot drop. A house materialized shortly after (I assume from a rare chest reward). My bank account stopped being negative and started behaving like it had finally learned discipline.

People ask me what changed. I tell them the truth: I didn’t change the game changed me. I didn’t “get back on my feet.” I respawned as a financially stable, emotionally balanced, highly optimized human being with upgraded stats in every category.

I owe everything to this game. Not just my success—but my entire reality rewrite. If I hadn’t played it, I can only assume I’d still be stuck in the tutorial of life, repeatedly dying to the same emotional boss fight with no save point in sight.
